event: Miracle of Saint Bernardino: The Child Born Dead Restored to Life
symbols: classical architectural setting — the public space where miracles become communal events
child brought back to life — divine power over death exercised through the saint
gathered witnesses — the community of faith testifying to God’s power
description: A vertically composed scene set within an elaborate classical architectural space featuring loggias, columns, and a courtyard. In the lower right portion, a group of richly dressed figures gathers, some gesturing or pointing. In the upper left, a figure stands at a balcony or window. The architecture dominates the composition with its geometric precision and perspective depth. The figures are relatively small within the grand setting. The palette is muted with earth tones and subtle color accents.

_meta.title: The Miracles of San Bernardino. Miracle baby born with one dead
